title: The $800 Repair
content: Car broke down. No emergency fund. Had to put the repair on a credit card. Paid interest for eight months. The repair was $600. I paid almost $800 by the time I was done. That's when I got serious about emergency savings. Never again.
extended_content: The worst part wasn't the money - it was the feeling of being trapped. Every month, watching that balance barely move because of interest. Knowing I was paying extra for something that already happened. That credit card became a reminder of what happens when you don't have a cushion. Now I tell everyone: emergency fund first. Before debt payoff. Before investing. First.
when_to_tell: when discussing emergency funds or unexpected expenses
lesson: Without an emergency fund, emergencies cost double
